edges.target: Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda
descriptions: M23 opposes the FDLR.
edges.evidence_quote: The M23 is made up primarily of Tutsis and opposes the Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda (FDLR)

edges.source: National Congress for the Defence of the People
edges.target: Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda
descriptions: CNDP aimed to neutralize the FDLR.
edges.evidence_quote: From 2004 to 2009, CNDP activities were justified, in part, by the need to neutralize the FDLR.

descriptions: Fidai Mahaz was an opponent of the Taliban from 2016 to 2021.
edges.evidence_quote: Non-state opponents ... Fidai Mahaz (sometimes 2016–2021, no fighting since 2021)

descriptions: IS-CAP emerged from the ADF.
edges.evidence_quote: It emerged out of the Ugandan Islamist group Allied Democratic Forces (ADF).

descriptions: Albanese allied with Cutellè and Tassone in the Laureana di Borrello feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni Novanta scoppia la faida di Laureana di Borrello tra gli Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club.description: The Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club is a one-percenter motorcycle club founded in San Bernardino, California on April 1, 1956. It expanded internationally, gaining notoriety in Australia, the United States, Germany, and Norway. The club is known for its violent criminal activities, including drug trafficking, homicide, and arson. In Australia, it is considered one of the most violent bikie gangs and was involved in the 2001 car bomb murder of former detective Don Hancock.

descriptions: Bandidos consider Gypsy Jokers as rivals in Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: while their rivals include the Comancheros, Finks, Gypsy Jokers, Hells Angels, Mongols, Notorious, Rebels and Red Devils

descriptions: The text lists the Gypsy Jokers as rivals of the Bandidos.
edges.evidence_quote: while their rivals include the Comancheros , Finks , Gypsy Jokers , Hells Angels , Mongols , Notorious , Rebels and Red Devils

descriptions: Gypsy Jokers members shot a Bandidos member in 2000.
edges.evidence_quote: Three men associated with the Adelaide chapter of the Gypsy Jokers – Robert Cameron, William James Fuller and David Shannon – were charged with Bobos' shooting.

descriptions: Feud between Bandidos and Gypsy Jokers in Hunter Valley in 2001.
edges.evidence_quote: A feud between the Bandidos and the Gypsy Jokers in the Hunter Valley began with several bashings before the home of a Bandidos member in Abermain was strafed in a drive-by shooting and a tattoo parlour owned by the club's Kurri Kurri chapter president Rodney Leslie "Pardo" Partington was firebombed in February 2001.

descriptions: Bandidos consider Gypsy Jokers as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: while their rivals include the ... Gypsy Jokers

descriptions: Club Deroes and Gypsy Jokers cooperated to eject the Mongrel Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 these four gangs combined forces to violently eject the New Zealand-based street gang, the Mongrel Mob which was attempting to establish itself in Perth.

descriptions: Gypsy Jokers and Coffin Cheaters cooperated to eject the Mongrel Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 these four gangs combined forces to violently eject the New Zealand-based street gang, the Mongrel Mob which was attempting to establish itself in Perth.

descriptions: Gypsy Jokers and Gods Garbage cooperated to eject the Mongrel Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 these four gangs combined forces to violently eject the New Zealand-based street gang, the Mongrel Mob which was attempting to establish itself in Perth.

descriptions: A Gypsy Joker member shot a former Hells Angel.
edges.evidence_quote: He shot and wounded a member of the Newboys gang and former Hells Angel outside their clubhouse in Adelaide, South Australia.

descriptions: Jesters MC is a support club for Gypsy Joker MC Norway.
edges.evidence_quote: Gypsy Joker MC Norway have a support club in Brazil, Jesters MC, established in 2017.

descriptions: Joker MC patched over to become Gypsy Joker MC Norway.
edges.evidence_quote: Just days after a big sign with Gypsy Joker MC decorated the former Joker MC clubhouse.

descriptions: Gypsy Joker MC amalgamated with Mandamas MC.
edges.evidence_quote: Following a friendly amalgamation with the notorious South Australian formed Mandamas MC and Gypsy Joker MC.

descriptions: Gypsy Jokers, along with three other clubs, cooperated to eject the Mongrel Mob from Perth.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989 these four gangs combined forces to violently eject the New Zealand-based street gang, the Mongrel Mob which was attempting to establish itself in Perth.

descriptions: A Gypsy Joker member shot a member of the Newboys gang.
edges.evidence_quote: He shot and wounded a member of the Newboys gang and former Hells Angel outside their clubhouse in Adelaide, South Australia.

descriptions: Gypsy Joker MC Australia was founded by former members of St Mary's Motorcycle Club.
edges.evidence_quote: In November 1969, Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club Australia was founded by former members of the St Mary's Motorcycle Club

descriptions: Infobox lists Gypsy Joker MC as ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Aryan Nations, Bandidos MC, Black Pistons MC, Breed MC, Buffalo crime family, Chicago Outfit, Detroit Partnership, Grim Reapers MC, Gypsy Joker MC, Hammerskins, New Orleans crime family, Pittsburgh crime family, Rock Machine MC

descriptions: The Outlaws are allied with the Gypsy Jokers.
edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws are allied with the Bandidos, the Black Pistons, the Breed, the Grim Reapers, and the Gypsy Jokers motorcycle gangs

descriptions: A Gypsy Joker member and a Rebels member were jointly involved in a homicide, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: On 14 April 2012, Anthony Perish (a Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club member), his brother Andrew (a Rebels Motorcycle Club member) and Matthew Lawton were sentenced to eighteen, nine and fifteen years respectively imprisonment for the homicide of convicted Sydney drug trafficker Terry Falconer, as well as firearms and drug dealing offences.

descriptions: The Vallelunga and Turrà were opposed to the Emanuele di Mongiana and their allies in the Faida dei boschi.
edges.evidence_quote: alleati dei Turrà, contrapporsi allo schieramento formato dagli Emanuele di Mongiana (intesi strazzi), agli Emanuele di Santa Caterina, ai Ciconte di Serra San Bruno e ai Nardo di Soriano

descriptions: Iozzo-Chiefari are subordinate to the Locale di Serra San Bruno.
edges.evidence_quote: Fanno capo al Locale di Serra San Bruno.

descriptions: Hells Angels absorbed the Popeyes of Montreal through a patch over.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels first entered Canada via a 'patch over' of the Popeyes of Montreal

Cártel de Tláhuac.description: Mexican criminal group founded in 2012 by Felipe de Jesús Pérez Luna, alias El Ojos, operating primarily in Tláhuac, Iztapalapa, Milpa Alta, Xochimilco, and the State of Mexico. It emerged from splinter groups of the Beltrán Leyva Cartel and engages in drug trafficking, homicide, extortion, kidnapping, vehicle theft, fuel theft, and human trafficking. The group is currently led by relatives of El Ojos and remains active.

descriptions: Cártel de Tláhuac was created from splintering of Cártel de los Beltrán Leyva.
edges.evidence_quote: Este grupo fue creado a partir de varias escisiones del Cártel de los Beltrán Leyva

descriptions: CJNG is listed as an ally of Cártel de Tláhuac in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: CJNG , Fuerza Anti-Unión , La Familia Michoacana , Células delictivas locales y de menor importancia como La Ronda 88 y Los Guerreros

edges.evidence_quote: I primi sono sostenuti anche dagli Avignone e i secondi dai Ligato- Pesce e i Piromalli .

descriptions: The Rocks Push fought with the Argyle Cut Push.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.

descriptions: Satanas is allied with Avenue King Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Avenue King Crips , Temple Street (gang)

Gopher Gang.description: The Gopher Gang was an early 20th-century New York street gang based in Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan. Formed in the 1890s from various local street gangs, it numbered around 500 members and controlled territory from Fourth to Forty-Second Street and Seventh to Eleventh Avenue. The gang engaged in burglary, armed robbery, assault, prostitution, and illegal gambling. It declined in the 1910s.

descriptions: The Five Points Gang was strongly rivaled by the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Five Points Gang (led by Paul Kelly) would rise to prominence during the early 1900s, strongly rivaled by the Hudson Dusters, the Gopher Gang, and others during the period.

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gopher Gang

edges.target: Battle Row Ladies' Social and Athletic Club
descriptions: The Battle Row Ladies' Social and Athletic Club was an ally of the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Battle Row Ladies' Social and Athletic Club , Parlor Mob , Gorillas , Rhodes Gang

descriptions: The Gorillas were an ally of the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Battle Row Ladies' Social and Athletic Club , Parlor Mob , Gorillas , Rhodes Gang

descriptions: The Hudson Dusters were rivals of the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Hudson Dusters

descriptions: The Parlor Mob was an ally of the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Battle Row Ladies' Social and Athletic Club , Parlor Mob , Gorillas , Rhodes Gang

descriptions: The Rhodes Gang was an ally of the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Battle Row Ladies' Social and Athletic Club , Parlor Mob , Gorillas , Rhodes Gang

descriptions: Infobox lists Gopher Gang as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Gopher Gang

descriptions: Hudson Dusters and Gopher Gang became allies during gang wars.
edges.evidence_quote: Knox, a former member of the Gopher Gang, had fled after a failed attempt to gain leadership of the gang from then-leader Marty Brennan. However, the two gangs later became allies during the gang wars against Gay Nineties gangs, the Potashes and Boodle Gangs

descriptions: The text states Neighbors' Sons were rivals of the Gopher Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: The Neighbors' Sons was a New York street gang which, operating in the neighborhoods between Bleecker and Grove Streets , were rivals of the Gopher Gang and the Hudson Dusters during the early 1900s.

Ryno-Skachevsky gang.description: Russian racist serial killer group composed of young men, active in the 2000s, headed by Artur Ryno and Pavel Skachevsky. The gang targeted and murdered immigrants from the Caucasus and Central Asia in Moscow. Members were convicted in 2008 for multiple murders and hate crimes.

descriptions: ISPP emerged from AKP.
edges.evidence_quote: It emerged in 2016 from local jihadist groups Maute Group, Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters (BIFF), and Ansar Khalifa Philippines (AKP).

edges.target: Communist Party of Peru – Huallaga Regional Committee
descriptions: PCP-CRH was a faction of Shining Path active in the Huallaga region.
edges.evidence_quote: the Communist Party of Peru – Huallaga Regional Committee ( PCP-CRH ) was active at the Huallaga region from 2004 until Comrade Artemio's capture in 2012.

edges.target: Communist Party of Peru – Huallaga Regional Committee
descriptions: PCP-CRH is a splinter group of Shining Path.
edges.evidence_quote: The three remaining splinter groups based themselves in the VRAEM area: ... - The "Communist Party of Peru – Huallaga Regional Committee" (PCP-CRH), a collective established in 2004 in the Huallaga Valley led by Comrade Artemio.

descriptions: Avignone allied with Mamone in Genoa during the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: negli anni '90 hanno operato nel traffico di droga a Genova e dove sono alleati dei Mamone

descriptions: The Eastside Mob was part of the Pascuzzi Combine organized by Catalanotte.
edges.evidence_quote: Catalanotte organized the remaining Mafia factions under a liquor combine that became known as the 'Pascuzzi Combine'.

descriptions: The Pascuzzi Combine was the forerunner of the Detroit Partnership.
edges.evidence_quote: The Pascuzzi Combine created a unified and cohesive criminal organization that controlled liquor smuggling, bootlegging, gambling, prostitution, narcotics and other rackets and was the forerunner of the Detroit Partnership under the future Zerilli-Tocco regime.

descriptions: Joseph Lonardo founded and led the Cleveland crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Joseph 'Big Joe' Lonardo became the first boss of the Cleveland crime family.

descriptions: Porrellos split from the Lonardos, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: the Porrellos broke away from the Lonardos and formed their own faction

descriptions: Booze Runners bombed Hell's Henchmen clubhouse as part of Outlaws' conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: On November 13, 1990, the Booze Runners, a gang founded and led by Kevin "Spike" O'Neill which was vying for chapter status in the Outlaws, bombed the Hell's Henchmen clubhouse in Rockford

descriptions: The Outlaws absorbed the Booze Runners to form their Stateline chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1991, the club also initiated its "Stateline" chapter in Antioch by absorbing the Booze Runners Motorcycle Club

descriptions: The UDA is listed as an opponent of the Provisional IRA in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: United Kingdom British Army , Royal Ulster Constabulary , Ulster loyalist paramilitaries Ulster Volunteer Force , Ulster Defence Association

Tonton Macoute.description: Haitian paramilitary and secret police force created in 1959 by dictator François Duvalier. Known for brutal state terrorism, assassinations, and human rights abuses, it suppressed political opposition through violence and intimidation. Formally disbanded in 1986, but members continued terrorizing the country.

edges.target: Front for the Advancement and Progress of Haïti
descriptions: FRAPH was a paramilitary group spawned from the Macoutes, described as modern Tonton Macoutes.
edges.evidence_quote: The most feared paramilitary group during the 1990s was the Front for the Advancement and Progress of Haïti (FRAPH), which Toronto Star journalist Linda Diebel described as modern Tonton Macoutes, and not the legitimate political party it claimed to be.

descriptions: Facchineri allied with Sorbara in San Giorgio Morgeto.
edges.evidence_quote: A San Giorgio Morgeto i Facchineri si alleano con i Valente (Giffone)-Mercuri-Ferraro (Melicucco) e poi con i Sorbara (San Giorgio) e i Ladini (Cinquefrondi)

descriptions: Tbilisi Clan is a major criminal clan within the Georgian mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: The Georgian mafia has two major criminal clans from Tbilisi and Kutaisi.

descriptions: Kutaisi and Tbilisi clans are in a violent feud.
edges.evidence_quote: the Georgian underworld in Russia, Georgia and elsewhere has been characterized by a violent feud between the Kutaisi and Tbilisi clans, led by Tariel Oniani and Aslan Usoyan, respectively.

descriptions: Young Gifteds is a rival of The Americans.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rivals of the Americans gang include all gangs affiliated to the British gang corporate body. These include the Hard Livings , 28s street gang, Junky Funky Kids, Ghetto Kids, Nice Time Kids, The Terrible West Siders, The Fancy Boys, 6bop gang, Mongrels, Young Gifteds and Terrible Josters.

Kizuna-kai.description: The Kizuna-kai is a yakuza organization based in Hyogo, Japan, formed on April 30, 2017 by defectors from the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi. It was originally named Ninkyo-dantai-Yamaguchi-gumi, then Ninkyo Yamaguchi-gumi, before adopting its current name in 2020. The group has about 50 members and 70 quasi-members, and is designated as a bōryokudan by the Hyogo Prefecture Public Safety Commission.

descriptions: Kizuna-kai and Ikeda-gumi are in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: These Yamaguchi-gumi and the three organizations that split from them are fighting each other.

descriptions: Infobox lists Ikeda-gumi as an ally of Kizuna-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Ikeda-gumi

descriptions: Keiji Union joined the Ninkyo-dantai-Yamaguchi-gumi (later Kizuna-kai).
edges.evidence_quote: it is believed that this group also joined the Ninkyo-dantai-Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Infobox lists Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi as a rival of Kizuna-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Kizuna-kai was formed by members of the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi, indicating a splinter relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: It was originally formed on April 30, 2017 by some direct members of the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi

descriptions: Kizuna-kai is a splinter group that split from Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Kizuna-kai split off from Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi in April 2017

descriptions: Infobox lists Yamaguchi-gumi as a rival of Kizuna-kai.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Yamaguchi-gumi

edges.evidence_quote: These Yamaguchi-gumi and the three organizations that split from them are fighting each other.

edges.evidence_quote: Kizuna-kai ... split

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi and Kizuna-kai are in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: These Yamaguchi-gumi and the three organizations that split from them are fighting each other.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi, Kizuna-kai, Ikeda-gumi

descriptions: Autodefensas de los Llanos Orientales merged to form AUC.
edges.evidence_quote: Al conformarse las AUC el 18 de abril de 1997, las Autodefensas Campesinas de Córdoba y Urabá, las del Magdalena Medio y las de los Llanos Orientales se unieron creando las Autodefensas Unidas de Colombia (AUC)

descriptions: D'Aquila and Masseria were rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: By 1920, D'Aquila's only significant rival was Giuseppe "Joe the Boss" Masseria.

descriptions: Masseria's gunmen assassinated D'Aquila.
edges.evidence_quote: On October 10, 1928, Masseria gunmen assassinated Salvatore D'Aquila outside his home.

descriptions: Masseria fought against Maranzano's Castellammarese gang.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1930, the Castellammarese War started between Masseria and Salvatore Maranzano, the new leader of Cola Schirò's Castellammarese gang.

Rebels Motorcycle Club.description: The Rebels Motorcycle Club is an Australian outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1969 by Clint Jacks in Brisbane, Queensland. At its peak, it was the largest motorcycle club in Australia with over 70 chapters and 1,000 members. The club is involved in drug trafficking, arms dealing, extortion, and other criminal activities, and is considered a criminal organization by Australian authorities.

descriptions: Bandidos consider Rebels as rivals in Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: while their rivals include the Comancheros, Finks, Gypsy Jokers, Hells Angels, Mongols, Notorious, Rebels and Red Devils

descriptions: The text lists the Rebels as rivals of the Bandidos.
edges.evidence_quote: while their rivals include the Comancheros , Finks , Gypsy Jokers , Hells Angels , Mongols , Notorious , Rebels and Red Devils

descriptions: Rebels members killed Bandidos members in 1997, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Bandidos members Michael "Kaos" Kulakowski, Saša "Sash" Milenković and Rick Raymond de Stoop were shot dead in the basement of a nightclub in Chippendale , Sydney in the early hours of 10 November 1997 by Konstantinos "Kon" Georgiou and Bruce Malcolm Harrison. Georgiou was a serving member of the Rebels at the time, while Harrison was a former member.

descriptions: Drive-by shootings in 2008 were linked to a dispute between Bandidos and Rebels.
edges.evidence_quote: Police believed the shootings, carried out in Lurnea and Sadleir , to be linked to a dispute between the Bandidos and Rebels.

descriptions: Bandidos firebombed a Rebels clubhouse in 2007 as retribution.
edges.evidence_quote: Bandidos Brisbane chapter president Blair Raymond Thomsen, vice-president Ivan Glavas, sergeant-at-arms Kenneth James Whittaker and John Debilla, an associate and disgruntled former Rebels member, were convicted in June 2008 of firebombing a Rebels clubhouse in Albion , Brisbane on 27 March 2007.

descriptions: Bandidos suspected of drive-by shootings to intimidate Rebels in 2017.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos are suspected in two drive-by shootings aimed at intimidation of Rebels members which were carried out in Bethania on 26 February 2017 and Calamvale on 10 March 2017.

descriptions: Rebels members shot a Bandidos member in 2019 during a pre-arranged meeting.
edges.evidence_quote: Seven Rebels members were charged after Harley Cranston, a member of the Bandidos' Beenleigh chapter who was allegedly brandishing a machete, was shot and wounded in the leg at the Logan Hyperdome in the Logan City suburb of Shailer Park on 4 February 2019.

descriptions: Bandidos consider Rebels as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: while their rivals include the ... Rebels

descriptions: Bandidos Perth chapter patched over to Rebels, indicating a transfer of membership.
edges.evidence_quote: The Perth Bandidos chapter patched over to the Rebels in June 2017 after senior Rebels members came to an amicable agreement with the Bandidos' eastern states -based leadership.

descriptions: Brother Speed allegedly sent methamphetamine to Rebels in Sydney.
edges.evidence_quote: The Brothers Speed in Idaho have allegedly been involved in sending methamphetamine, sealed inside the frames of motorcycles, to the Rebels in Sydney, Australia.

descriptions: Hells Angels in conflict with Rebels over colors.
edges.evidence_quote: The first conflict was with the Rebels after the Hells Angels demanded that the Rebels change their colors from red and white (the same color as the Hells Angels).

descriptions: Kings Crew and Rebels were allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Rebels Motorcycle Club

descriptions: A Loners chapter in Saskatoon patched over to the Rebels MC.
edges.evidence_quote: However, it would 'Patch-Over' to the Alberta-based Rebels MC.

descriptions: Morbids and Rebels were rival clubs that clashed.
edges.evidence_quote: The Morbids were later offered a path to Hells Angels membership on the condition that they merge with the Rebels, a rival club with whom they had previously clashed.

descriptions: Rebels associates killed a Bandidos member, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Two Rebel associates were arrested for the murder of Bandidos member Ross Brand after their clubhouse was raided, on 16 November 2008.

descriptions: A Gypsy Joker member and a Rebels member were jointly involved in a homicide, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: On 14 April 2012, Anthony Perish (a Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club member), his brother Andrew (a Rebels Motorcycle Club member) and Matthew Lawton were sentenced to eighteen, nine and fifteen years respectively imprisonment for the homicide of convicted Sydney drug trafficker Terry Falconer, as well as firearms and drug dealing offences.

descriptions: Rebels and Rock Machine engaged in a violent feud with firebombings, assaults, and assassination attempts.
edges.evidence_quote: The defection of Rebels MC members to the Rock Machine MC sparked an ongoing violent feud between the groups, when the Rock Machine settled in Perth in 2009 there were allegations by media that a turf war broke out between the two rival motorcycle clubs, with exchanges between the two groups including firebombings, assaults and the assassination attempt in 2011 of Rebels WA president Nick Martin, who survived being shot, tensions remain ongoing.

descriptions: Rock Machine and Rebels MC became rivals after Rock Machine expanded into Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: The rivalry between the Rock Machine and the Rebels Motorcycle Club started when the Canadian club expanded into Australia.

descriptions: Rebels attempted to recruit Tribesmen members through patching over.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2011, the newly established Rebels Motorcycle Club New Zealand Chapter voiced its intentions to 'patch over' the Tribesmen Motorcycle Club.

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham fought against Ghuraba al-Sham Front.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 2013, Ahrar al-Sham alongside Al-Nusra, ISIL, and the Tawhid Brigade fought the Ghuraba al-Sham Front because of looting and corruption on behalf of Ghuraba al-Sham as well as disputes Ghuraba al-Sham had with the Aleppo Sharia Court.

Ramacca Mafia family.description: The Ramacca Mafia family is a crime family of the Sicilian Cosa Nostra, operating primarily in the town of Ramacca, Sicily. Founded in the 1950s by Calogero 'Liddu' Conti, it engages in extortion, infiltration of public contracts, and political influence. The family has maintained control over local economic interests and alliances with powerful neighboring families. It remains active, with recent leadership transitions and ongoing criminal operations.

descriptions: The Ramacca Mafia family is listed as an ally of the Caltagirone Mafia family in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Catania Mafia family , Ramacca Mafia family , Corleonesi Mafia clan

descriptions: The Ramacca Mafia family is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Ramacca Mafia family

descriptions: Ramacca Mafia family is allied with Caltagirone Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Catania Mafia family , Caltagirone Mafia family , Corleone Mafia family

descriptions: Ramacca Mafia family is allied with Catania Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Catania Mafia family , Caltagirone Mafia family , Corleone Mafia family

descriptions: Ramacca Mafia family is allied with Corleone Mafia family.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Catania Mafia family , Caltagirone Mafia family , Corleone Mafia family

descriptions: Ramacca Mafia family head Calogero Conti had significant ties with the Corleonesi.
edges.evidence_quote: He also maintained significant ties with the Corleonesi

descriptions: Santapaola-Ercolano network has influence in Ramacca, implying cooperation with the local family.
edges.evidence_quote: The investigation revealed continued influence of the Santapaola-Ercolano network in Ramacca

descriptions: M-19 was allied with Alfaro Vive, Carajo!
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ¡Alfaro Vive, Carajo!

descriptions: Jaish-e-Fursan-e-Muhammad is listed as part of TTP's group in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Group: ... Jaish-e-Fursan-e-Muhammad

36 Boys.description: The 36 Boys were a gang of primarily Turkish immigrants based in Berlin-Kreuzberg, Germany, active from the late 1980s until the mid-1990s. Founded by Attila Murat Aydın, the gang engaged in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, arson, burglary, and vandalism. They marked their territory with graffiti and fought turf battles with Nazi and skinhead groups. After dissolution, some former members became social workers or pursued lawful careers.

descriptions: Warriors are a rival gang to the 36 Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Other rival gangs were the Warriors from the Schlesisches Tor and the Black Panthers from Wedding.

edges.source: Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubversivo de Colombia
descriptions: Remnants of ERPAC are possibly run by the Pachón Rozo family under the name Los Monroy.
edges.evidence_quote: Se presume que lo poco que quedó de la organización está en manos de la familia Pachón Rozo ... haciendosen conocer como Los Monroy
